overview.text: OpenAI was founded in December 2015 in San Francisco as a nonprofit AI research lab, backed by Elon Musk, Sam Altman and other early investors, with a stated goal of advancing AI for the benefit of humanity rather than financial return. It has since restructured around a capped-profit and then for-profit arm while building the GPT model family and ChatGPT.

citations.quote: Our goal is to advance digital intelligence

what_theyve_done.text: OpenAI launched in December 2015 as a nonprofit with roughly $1 billion pledged by Musk, Altman, Greg Brockman, Reid Hoffman, Peter Thiel, Amazon Web Services and others.

citations.quote: unconstrained by a need to generate financial return

what_theyve_done.text: It released GPT-2 in 2019, GPT-3 in 2020, and then ChatGPT in November 2022, which became the fastest-growing consumer software product in history at the time, followed by GPT-4 in March 2023.

citations.quote: gaining over 100 million users in two months

what_theyve_done.text: In June 2026 it began a limited preview of its GPT-5.6 family, led by the flagship model Sol, alongside mid-tier Terra and fast, low-cost Luna, with an initial rollout restricted to a small group of trusted partners because of the family's elevated cybersecurity capability.

citations.quote: find vulnerabilities and pieces of exploits

strengths.text: OpenAI's own GPT-5.6 system card rates the family's biological and chemical capability as High and reports it beating expert baselines on some wet-lab troubleshooting evaluations.
It layers real-time activation-classifier monitoring and trust-based access programs on top of its models for its highest-risk capability domains rather than withholding a model outright.

citations.quote: making malicious use at scale harder

current_focus.text: OpenAI's current public focus is the GPT-5.6 family, led by Sol, which its own preview documentation says pushes coding, biology and cybersecurity capability further than any prior OpenAI model and adds new higher-effort reasoning and multi-subagent modes for long, complex problems, paired with a hardened safety stack.

roadmap.text: OpenAI says it plans broader availability of GPT-5.6 beyond the initial trusted-partner preview once its layered safeguards for the family's higher-risk capabilities are in place.
